title: Hugelia floccosa
taxonomy: Hugelia floccosa is classified within the family Calycanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Hugelia, which is a small group of flowering plants. This plant is native to South America, specifically in the Andes mountain range. Hugelia floccosa is an evergreen shrub with unique, fragrant flowers. It is a relatively rare and understudied species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Hugelia floccosa, a plant native to South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
